picks.rationale: The Pistons, as the top seed (60-22), hold a significant home-court advantage, having won three of their four playoff games at home. The Cavaliers, in contrast, struggled on the road in their first-round series, losing all three away games against Toronto. Detroit also won two of three regular season matchups against Cleveland in 2026, showcasing their ability to compete effectively. With a strong defense and rebounding edge, coupled with Cade Cunningham's exceptional form, the Pistons are well-positioned to cover this modest spread.

Both teams are coming off grueling seven-game series, which can sometimes lead to less stringent defense and higher scoring. The Cavaliers have averaged 116.5 points per game in their last 10 outings, while the Pistons have averaged 109.6 points per game over the same span. In their last five head-to-head regular season games, the total exceeded 215.5 in three contests, indicating a propensity for higher-scoring affairs. With key offensive talents like Cade Cunningham for Detroit and Donovan Mitchell and James Harden for Cleveland, offensive firepower is expected to drive the score over the total.

picks.game: Los Angeles Lakers @ Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: The Lakers are severely hampered by the absence of superstar Luka Doncic, who is out with a hamstring injury and averaged 33.5 points, 7.7 rebounds, and 8.3 assists in the regular season. The Thunder, the Western Conference's top seed (64-18), swept the Lakers 4-0 in the regular season, winning by an overwhelming average margin of 29.3 points. Oklahoma City has been dominant recently, boasting an 8-2 record in their last 10 games with an impressive +14.9 point differential. With a deeper roster and playing at home where they push the tempo, the Thunder are poised for a commanding victory.

picks.game: Los Angeles Lakers @ Oklahoma City Thunder
picks.rationale: The Oklahoma City Thunder possess a high-octane offense, ranking 5th in the league with 119.0 points per game and averaging 123.7 points over their last 10 contests. They consistently play at a fast pace, especially at home, which typically results in higher-scoring games. In their regular season sweep of the Lakers, the Thunder scored 123, 139, 119, and 121 points, demonstrating their offensive potency against this opponent. Even with Luka Doncic out for the Lakers, the Thunder's offensive prowess and rapid pace are strong indicators that the game total will exceed 213.5.

The Detroit Pistons finished as the Eastern Conference's top seed (60-22) and demonstrated significant resilience by overcoming a 3-1 deficit in their first-round series. They possess a strong home-court advantage, having secured three victories in their four playoff games played at home. Conversely, the Cleveland Cavaliers struggled noticeably on the road in their previous series, losing all three away games. With Cade Cunningham's recent stellar performances and the Pistons' overall defensive strength, Detroit is well-positioned to earn a straight-up victory at home.

The Colorado Avalanche are a dominant force at home, boasting a 55-16-11 regular season record and a commanding 9-6 victory in Game 1 of this series. The Minnesota Wild are significantly hampered by injuries to key defensemen Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin, which was evident in their defensive struggles in the opener. Public and sharp money have consistently supported the Avalanche, with the moneyline moving further in their favor, indicating strong confidence in their ability to win by multiple goals.

The Colorado Avalanche are the superior team, holding a 1-0 series lead and an impressive 55-16-11 regular season record, finishing first in the Western Conference. Their home advantage at Ball Arena is significant, and they face a Minnesota Wild team missing two crucial defensemen in Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin. The moneyline has seen consistent movement towards the Avalanche, reflecting strong market confidence in their outright victory.

Despite an anomalous 9-6 Game 1, a regression to typical playoff scoring is highly anticipated for Game 2, especially given the defensive capabilities of both teams. The Colorado Avalanche finished the regular season with the fewest goals against in the league, and all their first-round games saw six or fewer goals. While Minnesota's defense is hampered by injuries, their regular season metrics show a strong defensive structure, and both starting goalies are generally reliable.

Despite a lopsided Game 1 loss, the Minnesota Wild demonstrated significant offensive capability by scoring six goals against a top-tier Avalanche defense. Historically, the Wild have shown they can compete with Colorado, winning their regular season series 2-1-1 and holding a 3-2 record in their last five head-to-head matchups. With the +1.5 spread, Minnesota only needs to lose by one goal or win outright, which is a strong possibility if they can make defensive adjustments and capitalize on their offensive output.

Game 1 exploded for 15 goals, highlighting the immense offensive firepower of both teams, particularly the Colorado Avalanche who led the league in goals scored during the regular season. Minnesota's defensive injuries to Joel Eriksson Ek and Jonas Brodin further compromise their ability to contain Colorado's potent attack, and the Wild themselves proved they can contribute significantly to the score. The favorable odds for the Over at -105 present value, anticipating another high-event game.

Miami's Sandy Alcantara (3-2, 3.04 ERA) is having an impressive season, ranking in the top-40 in ERA and WHIP, and the Marlins are 4-3 in his starts. In contrast, Baltimore's Chris Bassitt (2-2, 5.46 ERA) has struggled significantly, allowing 17 earned runs in 28 innings this year, and the Orioles are on a five-game losing streak. The betting market has shown firm support for Miami, with the moneyline moving from -121 to -131, and public betting percentages climbing to 84% on the Marlins. Miami also holds a 4-2 head-to-head record against Baltimore in the last three seasons and is currently on a two-game winning streak against them.

The Chicago Cubs enter this game on a six-game winning streak, while the Cincinnati Reds have dropped four in a row. The pitching matchup heavily favors the Cubs, with Jameson Taillon (1.15 WHIP) taking the mound against Andrew Abbott, who has struggled to limit baserunners. The market has shown steady support for the Cubs, with their moneyline tightening from -163 to -168, indicating confidence in their ability to win convincingly. Given the Cubs' superior recent form, pitching advantage, and offensive depth, they are well-positioned to cover the run line at home.

A projection system indicates a 'STRONG UNDER' for this game, with a projected total of 7.1 runs against the verified line of 8.5. This significant difference suggests a clear edge on the Under. The starting pitching matchup features Jacob deGrom for the Rangers and Elmer Rodriguez for the Yankees, both capable of suppressing offense. Betting on the Under 8.5 capitalizes on the expectation of fewer runs than the market currently implies, offering plus money value.

picks.game: Chicago White Sox @ Los Angeles Angels
picks.rationale: The Chicago White Sox have a strong recent head-to-head record against the Los Angeles Angels, winning their last three matchups on May 4, April 29, and April 28. In the last three seasons, the White Sox hold a dominant 10-5 record against the Angels. The Angels have also shown struggles when favored, with a 2-3 record when the moneyline is -116 or more this season. Given the White Sox's recent dominance in this specific matchup and the Angels' struggles as favorites, the White Sox moneyline at -104 presents good value.

The Arizona Diamondbacks have a significant pitching advantage with Eduardo Rodriguez (2-0, 3.03 ERA) starting, who has been a stabilizing force for the team. In contrast, the Pirates are starting Bubba Chandler (1-3, 4.97 ERA), who has struggled with efficiency, allowing nine runs over his last nine innings. The Diamondbacks are motivated to snap a four-game losing streak at home, while the Pirates are on a three-game winning streak, making this a classic 'buy low, sell high' spot. The betting market has shown movement towards the Diamondbacks moneyline, reflecting confidence in their ability to secure a home victory.

Arsenal enters this crucial second leg with a strong home record in the Champions League, remaining unbeaten at the Emirates and conceding no goals in the knockout stages. The return of key attacking players Martin Odegaard and Kai Havertz significantly boosts their offensive capabilities. Furthermore, the moneyline movement from initial offerings around -163 to the current -180 suggests sharp money is backing Arsenal. Atlético Madrid also faces a disadvantage after their team's sleep was disrupted by fireworks outside their hotel the night before the match.

The line movement for the total has shifted towards the Over 2.5, moving from -108 to -120, indicating increased betting confidence in a higher-scoring game. The first leg between these two teams ended in a 1-1 draw, demonstrating both sides' ability to find the back of the net. Arsenal's attack is bolstered by the return of Martin Odegaard and Kai Havertz, enhancing their goal-scoring potential. Additionally, Atlético Madrid has shown strong offensive form recently, scoring 11 goals in their last five domestic fixtures.

Viktor Gyökeres is a prime candidate to score, having already converted a penalty in the first leg of this semi-final tie. He is Arsenal's leading goalscorer in the Premier League with 14 goals, showcasing his consistent threat in front of goal. The return of creative midfielders Martin Odegaard and Kai Havertz is expected to generate more scoring opportunities for Arsenal's forwards. Atlético Madrid also has some minor defensive injury concerns with Jose Gimenez and David Hancko listed as minor doubts, potentially creating vulnerabilities for Gyökeres to exploit.

Kai Havertz is confirmed to be available and in the squad for Arsenal, marking a significant boost to their attacking options. His presence is expected to enhance Arsenal's attacking fluidity and provide another potent threat in the final third. Atlético Madrid's defense may be slightly compromised due to minor injury doubts for key defenders Jose Gimenez and David Hancko, as well as the absence of midfielders Pablo Barrios and Nico Gonzalez. Playing at home, Arsenal's offense is expected to be aggressive, providing Havertz with ample opportunities to score.

Antoine Griezmann is a crucial attacking force for Atlético Madrid and is confirmed to be active for this high-stakes Champions League semi-final. He has been a prolific scorer for his club this season, netting 16 goals in La Liga. Atlético Madrid's recent offensive form has been strong, with the team scoring 11 goals in their last five domestic matches, indicating their ability to challenge Arsenal's defense. In a tightly contested semi-final, key players like Griezmann often rise to the occasion to make a decisive impact.
